headlines.description: Marcus Rashford scored for the first time since March in Manchester United's 3-0 Premier League victory at Southampton on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rashford on target as Utd win 3-0 at St. Mary's

events.name: Ipswich Town at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Brighton kept up their unbeaten start to the season but dropped their first home points as a plucky Ipswich Town held out for a fortunate goalless draw in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Plucky Ipswich earn draw at unbeaten Brighton

headlines.description: Crystal Palace's Jean-Philippe Mateta scored twice including a last-gasp penalty as they clawed their way back from two goals down to salvage a 2-2 Premier League draw against promoted Leicester City at Selhurst Park on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Mateta penalty saves Palace against Leicester

headlines.description: A late Danny Ings goal snatched an unlikely 1-1 draw for West Ham United at Fulham in their Premier League derby at Craven Cottage on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ings scores late as West Ham draw with Fulham

headlines.description: A superb second-half goal from substitute Callum Hudson-Odoi gave Nottingham Forest the biggest surprise win of the Premier League season so far as they beat Liverpool 1-0 at Anfield on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool stunned by Forest at Anfield for 1st loss

headlines.description: Manchester City beat Brentford 2-1 at home on Saturday to maintain their perfect start to the Premier League season after Erling Haaland netted twice following the fastest goal of the season from the visitors.
headlines.shortLinkText: Haaland strikes twice as Man City come from behind to beat Brentford

headlines.description: Jhon Duran scored a brilliant long-range winner as Aston Villa came from two goals down to beat Everton 3-2 in a hugely entertaining Premier League clash on Saturday, as striker Ollie Watkins opened his account for the season with a brace.
headlines.shortLinkText: Duran stunner sees Villa edge Everton in thriller

headlines.description: Chelsea substitute Christopher Nkunku struck in the 86th minute to secure a 1-0 victory at Bournemouth in a game littered with a Premier League record 14 yellow cards on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sub Nkunku nets late Chelsea winner at B'mouth
